scope.summary: Define a GarmentParticleView type as an alternative read-only projection of a simulated GarmentSpec: maps each panel to a set of 3D world-space particles from the SolverResult, enabling 3D-first editing commands (move particles, add pinch constraints) that propagate back to 2D panel vertices via an unfurl pass
acceptance_criteria: GarmentParticleView struct is defined in tailor-solver/src/spec.rs: panel_id, particles_3d: Vec<[f32;3]> (world-space cm), face_indices: Vec<[u32;3]>, and a correspondence_map linking each particle index to its panel vertex index in GarmentSpec
A get_garment_particle_view MCP tool returns the GarmentParticleView for a simulated (status=simulated) draft garment

An edit_garment_3d MCP tool accepts a list of (particle_index, new_position_cm: [f32;3]) displacements and back-projects them to 2D panel vertex adjustments using the correspondence_map; the result is applied as a JSON Merge Patch via the existing edit_garment path
The back-projection uses the same ARAP flatten pass as the UV-from-pattern group (UvTexture MT-213) to maintain 2D panel validity after 3D edits
3D edits are CRDT-recorded as TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded events on the affected panels, transparent to the model
The symmetric view is regenerated after every simulate_garment call and cached in the SolverResult for the lifetime of the simulation run
proof_targets: cargo test -p tailor-solver particle_view_correspondence (every GarmentParticleView particle maps back to a valid panel vertex in the source GarmentSpec)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::edit_garment_3d_back_projects (3D displacement on particle produces valid updated PanelSpec vertices_cm via ARAP unfurl)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::edit_garment_3d_emits_crdt_event (3D edit emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded for all affected panels)
scope.risk_if_missed: Tailor is 2D-panel-first only. Models and operators cannot adjust a garment by touching its 3D drape surface (e.g., 'pull this side seam out by 2 cm in 3D space'). This blocks the most intuitive class of drape corrections — adjusting where cloth falls on the body by interacting with the 3D result, not the 2D pattern. As Garment Particles and similar 2D-3D symmetric representations mature, Tailor cannot participate in the 3D-edit mode without this foundation.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
